tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The tests execute Go tests via subprocess (go test -v -run TestName), compile the code (go build), and run go vet. They verify actual execution behavior, not just text presence. While test_sanitize_file_exists and test_sanitize_test_file_exists check file existence, the majority of tests execute code.

A stub returning nil or the original error would fail TestSanitizeError_SimpleURLError since the Go test checks that the sanitized error message contains [REDACTED] instead of the original secret.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names (test_sanitize_error_simple_url, test_sanitize_error_wrapped, etc.) and include docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces Go source code, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.
